goal: Build a pick-from-pile station: 3 cubes pre-stacked vertically at one xy position. Robot picks topmost cube first, then middle, then bottom — destacking. Tests handler picking cubes at varying z heights from a static pile.
thoughts:
Six pick-from-pile specifics for CP-31 (extends CP-27 — non-conveyor source, but vertically stacked):
1) Cubes pre-stacked at xy=(0.4, 0.3): Cube_1 at z=0.835 (bottom), Cube_2 at z=0.885 (middle), Cube_3 at z=0.935 (top).
2) WorkSurface (stationary belt, surface_velocity=[0.001,0,0]) under the pile — handler-required belt_path. Pile sits on it.
3) Robot picks closest-z-by-reach. _next_cube selects cubes within 0.95m horizontal AND base_z-0.30 < cube_z < base_z+0.50. All 3 cubes within range (z=0.83-0.94 vs robot base z=0.75 → +0.085 to +0.185).
4) cuRobo's planner descends to cube z. For top cube z=0.935 → fingers contact at z=0.935-0.025-0.105-0.005 = 0.80. EE at h_mid_pick=0.935+0.18=1.115m before descent. Within reach.
5) Drop into bin behind robot.
6) Order: controller picks WHICHEVER CUBE'S xy is closest to sensor first. All 3 at same xy → controller picks first one in source_paths order = Cube_1. But Cube_1 is BOTTOM — picking it would destabilize Cube_2/3 above. Real test of handler's cube-selection: with all at same xy, source_paths order matters.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Stationary work surface under pile
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/WorkSurface",
    position=[0.4, 0.3, 0.78],
    size=[0.30, 0.30, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.001, 0, 0],
)

# 3 cubes stacked vertically at (0.4, 0.3): bottom Cube_1 at z=0.835, middle Cube_2 at z=0.885, top Cube_3 at z=0.935
# Note: source_paths order is Cube_3 (top) first → handler picks top first, then middle, then bottom
for i, z in enumerate([0.835, 0.885, 0.935]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[0.4, 0.3, z], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(3)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(3)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.3, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# source_paths order matters — pick top cube first to avoid destabilizing pile
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/WorkSurface",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_3", "/World/Cube_2", "/World/Cube_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/WorkSurface", "/World/Bin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-31 demonstrates destacking — picking from a vertical pile, top-down. source_paths order [Cube_3, Cube_2, Cube_1] tells handler to pick top first. Tests cube-selection logic when multiple cubes share xy. Industrial use: bin-picking from a heap, palette destacking.
failure_modes: Bottom cube picked first → destabilizes pile, top cubes fall. Solution: source_paths order = top-first.
Cube_2/Cube_3 unstable on Cube_1 (cube-on-cube precision). Cubes are 5cm cubes flush stacked — should hold under gravity.
Robot can't reach z=0.935 vertical lift — within Franka's 0.85m reach (0.935-0.75=0.185m vertical, 0.4m horizontal, sqrt=0.44m total). Within reach.
Sensor at (0.4, 0.3, 0.835) — covers bottom cube xy. Top cube z=0.935 above sensor. Sensor still triggers when any cube within bbox xy. Adequate.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (180s sim — orig 90s. cube_final=(-0.02, -0.40, 0.785) in Bin)
